threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to complain about the 'charm' of the old houses while hearing sirens every night. That’s a rookie mistake.
Last night on the 3 bus, a guy serenaded the whole ride with karaoke. Best show I've seen in ages, even the driver grinned!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a family using a rollator and adapting their route around broken pavement while sharing jokes. Community in motion.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you have a pop-up vegan café in an old laundromat, where laundry machines double as seating. That's quintessential Berlin spirit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here if they know which taco truck has the best salsa—and it ain’t the one everyone posts on Instagram.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy singing opera on the N-Judah this morning. Totally nails the quirky spirit of Muni—only in SF can you go from chaos to a solo concert in seconds.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how quiet Gràcia gets after 10 PM. The bustling plazas empty out, revealing a different, peaceful side of the neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them trying to navigate the winding streets while holding a paper map, oblivious to how everyone else uses Google Maps.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop for the red lights on Mare Street, while locals just zip across like it’s a game of Frogger.
Caught a night bus through Brixton. Saw a guy trying to impress a girl by casually juggling fruit. She just rolled her eyes, classic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler negotiating with a street vendor for a pretzel in perfect German, I knew this was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you get spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings—all while your neighbor dog-walks with a beer in hand.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they can name the taqueria that had the best burritos before the last renovation. The old spots still matter.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 Mission with someone singing to their Bluetooth speaker. Only in SF do you get live music and a quick commute all at once.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for bars and nightlife, but the mornings here are for families and fresh bread from local forners. It’s a different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they’re still trying to figure out the bus timetable instead of just using Google Maps. Old habits die hard.
